_meta.note: FR-only mutation suggestions for CMC optimization. CDR residues are advisory-only and must not receive automated substitution lists.

action_high.suggestion: Replace surface-exposed K/R in FR regions with Q/E/N to lower pI toward p25–p75 range (6.85–8.74).
action_high.rationale: High pI (>p95=9.09) increases risk of non-specific binding and self-association at physiological pH.

action_low.suggestion: Replace surface D/E in FR regions with N/Q to raise pI toward p5–p95 range (5.20–9.09).
action_low.rationale: Low pI (<p5=5.20) may reduce protein stability and expression yield.

action_high.suggestion: Replace hydrophobic FR residues (I/L/V/F) with polar equivalents (T/S/N/Q) to reduce GRAVY.
action_high.rationale: High GRAVY (>p95=-0.19) indicates excessive hydrophobicity, elevating aggregation and HIC retention risk.

action_low.suggestion: No automated hydrophobic-core enrichment is recommended from sequence score alone; review structure and expression context before considering FR changes.
action_low.rationale: Very low GRAVY may indicate poor expression or stability in hydrophobic regions.

action_high.suggestion: Avoid D/E-K/R adjacent pairs and W/Y in FR loops. Replace instability-contributing dipeptides (DP, DG, WP) with stable alternatives.
action_high.rationale: Instability index >p95 (47.1) predicts reduced protein stability. Target: p25–p75 (33.2–41.6).

action_low.suggestion: No action needed — low instability index is favorable.
action_low.rationale: Instability index below p5 (27.7) indicates high stability.

action_high.suggestion: Replace FR K/R residues with Q/N or introduce conservative D/E to reduce positive charge. Avoid positions involved in antigen binding.
action_high.rationale: Excessive positive net charge (>p95=5.9) is associated with non-specific tissue binding and polyreactivity.

action_low.suggestion: Replace FR D/E with N/Q to reduce negative charge. Avoid CDR residues.
action_low.rationale: Strong negative net charge (<p5=-3.4) may reduce expression and solubility in standard conditions.

action_high.suggestion: Identify 9-mer window with highest hydrophobic fraction. Replace 1-2 hydrophobic FR residues (L/I/V/F/W) with T/S/N to reduce patch. Avoid CDR positions.
action_high.rationale: Hydrophobic patch >p95 (0.78) is a key predictor of aggregation propensity and viscosity.

action_high.suggestion: Neutralize the most charged 7-mer window. Replace 1-2 K/R or D/E residues in FR with neutral residues (Q/N/A).
action_high.rationale: Dense charge patches (>p95=4.0) can cause non-specific electrostatic interactions.

action_high.suggestion: Introduce T/S/N substitutions at hydrophobic runs (≥3 consecutive AILMFWV). Target FR positions first, especially in FR2/FR3.
action_high.rationale: Multiple aggregation motifs (>p95=2.0) predict high risk during manufacturing and storage.

action_high.suggestion: For FR liabilities only: consider conservative motif cleanup after protected-position filtering. CDR NG/NS findings are advisory-only and require structural and functional validation.
action_high.rationale: Multiple deamidation sites (>p95=3.0) indicate chemical instability risk during storage.

action_high.suggestion: For FR liabilities only: consider conservative motif cleanup after protected-position filtering. CDR DG/DS findings are advisory-only and require structural and functional validation.
action_high.rationale: Multiple isomerization sites (>p95=3.0) indicate aspartate rearrangement risk affecting potency.
